Build succeeds. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`index.css`** - Added a proper height chain (`h-full` = `height: 100%`) from `html` → `body` → `#root`. This ensures the viewport height flows down through the DOM so the dashboard layout fills exactly the viewport and nothing more.

**`DashboardLayout.tsx`** - Three changes:
1. Changed `h-screen` to `h-full` on the root container - now uses the height chain instead of a hardcoded `100vh`, which plays better with the constrained parent hierarchy
2. Added `min-h-0` to the content wrapper div - prevents the flex child from growing beyond its parent when content is tall (overrides the default `min-height: auto` behavior in flexbox)
3. Added `min-h-0` to `<main>` - same reason: ensures the scrollable area can shrink to fit its allocated space rather than expanding to fit its content

The core issue was that without a height chain, the `#root` div had no height constraint, so when page content exceeded the viewport, the entire layout could grow beyond the intended viewport boundary. Now the height is properly constrained at every level, and `min-h-0` ensures flex children respect those constraints.
